body.title: CLOSED: TASK_SET short TTL + upsert
body.result: TASK_SET upsert (twm_clear_task_set before push) + TTL 600s; HeartbeatSource+MilieuSource already at 600s
body.description:
TASK_SET observations accumulate across a session (5+ copies visible in search) because each action_request intent pushes a new one without expiring the old. The TTL is set to _nexus_twm_ttl() (session-length). These exist for restart-rebuild (letting Igor reconstruct context after crash), so their TTL should match the restart window (5-10 min), not the session. After that window they're stale — the user has moved on. Same issue applies to other periodic push sources (HeartbeatSource ticks, MilieuSource snapshots).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/main.py (TASK_SET push at ~line 3867-3878, HIGH inertia — pre-approved); wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources.py (HeartbeatSource, MilieuSource TTLs)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; safeguards (main.py HIGH inertia — pre-approved inline)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: (1) change TASK_SET push TTL to 600s; (2) add clear-existing-TASK_SET step before pushing new one for same thread (upsert behavior); (3) audit HeartbeatSource and MilieuSource for appropriate short TTLs. Out of scope: changing goal extraction logic; changing urgency/salience values; removing TASK_SET mechanism.
**Test plan:** Send two action_request turns in sequence. Assert only one TASK_SET observation exists in TWM for that thread after the second turn. Assert TASK_SET TTL expires before 15 minutes. Verify restart-rebuild path still works when Igor restarts within the TTL window.
**Pass condition:** After 10 minutes of idle, twm_read() for category=task_set returns empty. During an active conversation, only one TASK_SET exists per thread at any time.
